---
title: "Documentation"
---

## Getting Started

SimiC-Suite contains two complementary packages:

::: {.package-tabs .panel-tabset}

## SimiCPipeline

::: {.package-panel .pipeline-panel}

Use SimiCPipeline when the goal is to infer phenotype-specific gene regulatory networks from single-cell RNA-seq data and compute Regulon Activity Score (RAS) values.

| Step | Purpose |
|---|---|
| Data preparation | Use single-cell RNA-seq counts and phenotype labels. |
| GRN inference | Learn phenotype-specific regulatory networks. |
| RAS calculation | Calculate Regulon Activity Score (RAS) values for downstream interpretation. |

[Open SimiCPipeline repository](https://github.com/ML4BM-Lab/SimiCPipeline){.btn .btn-primary}

:::

## SimiCviz

::: {.package-panel .viz-panel}

Use SimiCviz when the goal is to assess, compare, and visualize GRN outputs in R / Bioconductor workflows. SimiCviz is designed to be tool-agnostic, so it can support outputs from different GRN inference approaches.

| Step | Purpose |
|---|---|
| GRN assessment | Inspect inferred network weights and summary statistics. |
| RAS visualization | Compare Regulon Activity Score (RAS) patterns across labels and cell types. |
| Reporting | Generate reproducible tables and figures for downstream analysis. |

[Open SimiCviz repository](https://github.com/ML4BM-Lab/SimiCviz){.btn .btn-danger}

:::

:::

## Suggested Workflow

1. Prepare single-cell RNA-seq data and phenotype annotations.
2. Run SimiCPipeline for phenotype-specific GRN inference.
3. Calculate Regulon Activity Score (RAS) values with SimiCPipeline.
4. Use SimiCviz to assess and visualize GRN outputs.
